section_title: OTE PATTERN RECOGNITION SERIES AND DAILY CHART ANALYSIS
content:
Welcome back. This is video number 13 in a continuing series of the Optimal Trade Entry pattern
recognition series for the Inner Circle Trader YouTube channel. Our example again today is the Dollar CAD
and our charts are already trained in on the 5-minute chart. It is advised to take a look at your daily chart
and look at the most recent optimal trade entry that formed which led to this strong breakdown on a daily
chart.
When we see big moves like that, the natural assumption is that it's gone down too far and it's going to
have to reverse. Many times that's going to plague your trust in a pattern like this. In the New York session,
as you can see, the market has been going down. When we add the annotations to the chart, you'll see
that nothing really has changed; it's still the same logic. You can't just simply say the market's oversold, and
the pattern can't work; that's not how this works. The market's going to seek liquidity and it's going to
expand and seek that liquid above or below the marketplace. If you look at the daily chart, it had a really
obvious equal lows. I teach that as Candyland; it's just ripe for the taking sell-side liquidity. Couple that with
Optimal Trade Entry on a daily chart, and you get beautiful results like this.

Alright, so now I'm going to add annotations. You can see the New York session again, standard times
never changing: 8:30 a.m. New York time to 11:00 a.m. The Optimal Trade Entry is here. Obviously, you can
see that the market has been going down, and we had this retracement in here inside the heart of the New
York session that trades back to the Optimal Trade Entry. Notice it goes just a little bit above that; that's
okay, it's fine, it's not a big deal.

section_title: SELECTING THE CORRECT SWING HIGH FOR OTE
content:
You may have looked at this and elected to use this swing high here. I used this one only because it has
the direct displacement from this high starting going lower. It did have a higher high here, and I'll show you
what that looks like in a moment, but drop down, then consolidation, and then run more one more time rally
up. To me, I'm going to use this point here. Before I go through this one, I've got to take the annotations off
for a moment. You can see this one's slightly higher than this one, but this one's where all the volume is.
You see that with body candles versus just wick.

Now adding that 62 percent retracing level, that's our standard for this model as an energy pattern. Your
stop would have to be above the short-term high, whatever fits your risk appetite
- if it's 1 percent, half a
percent, 2 percent, or 20 percent if you don't plan to be in the business too long.
The short-term low trades to 38.11 and a half, so that gives you about what, 20 pips or so, about 20 pips
just to the old low here.
And then you get:
- Half a standard deviation at 39.93.
- One full standard deviation at 37.75 and a half.
- One and a half standard deviations at 1.3757 and a half.
The low of the day comes in exactly at 1.3757 and a half.

So beautiful, beautiful symmetry in the Dollar CAD. Obvious setup from the daily chart using the things that
I've taught, obviously in this channel for free. If you go through all the tutorials and see everything I've
shown in those videos coupled with this Optimal Trade Entry pattern recognition series, you can see how
the things that I teach and I look for appear every single market day in this time of day. So this is an
example again you can use for your edification. Hope you find it insightful. Until next time, wish you good
luck and get ready.
